name: Pete Candoli
abstract: Walter Joseph "Pete" Candoli (June 28, 1923 – January 11, 2008) was an American jazz trumpeter. He played with the big bands of Woody Herman and Stan Kenton and worked in the studios of the recording and television industries.

interviews.title: Pete Candoli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

works.t: Strike Up the Band: The Man I Love

concerts.label: Woody Herman and His Thundering Herd: 40th Anniversary Concert
JVC Jazz Festival: The Legacy of Woody Herman

about_quotes.speaker: Jake Hanna
about_quotes.evidence: We were on a ship, Carl Fontana and him and Pete, his brother Pete, and Bob Cooper was alive then, Bob was playing, myself, Randy Sandke, Ross Tompkins.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jake Hanna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Sheldon
about_quotes.evidence: I remember, and Pete Candoli, we worked together on Merv’s show for like twenty years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Sheldon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: DeFranco Buddy
about_quotes.evidence: A lot of good players, there again, in that band: Hal Schaffer and Pete Candoli.
about_quotes.doc_title: DeFranco Buddy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Collette
about_quotes.evidence: Even Pete Candoli who was a great, great trumpet player, maybe he had an act though, he would maybe stand outside the door until it was about five minutes of and then walk in, you know, because he always wanted to be kind of a star.
about_quotes.doc_title: Buddy Collette interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Findley
about_quotes.evidence: sitting next to ... Conte Candoli, Pete Candoli ... just so many players that I worked with all the time that I mean I’m talking about a daily basis I’d work with these people.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chuck Findley interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 6, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Duffy Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: My father and I were appearing with Edie Adams and her husband Pete Candoli at the time
about_quotes.doc_title: Duffy Jackson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 17, 1995
